_source: Uniclass 2015 Table SL — Hospitality rooms (verbatim from primary NBS Source mirror declared in sprint-Z-source-provenance.md §1, supplemented by buildig/uniclass-2015 community CSV v1.22)
_note: Per-entry _verification_status records sourcing. Uniclass SL has NO standalone hospitality branch — hotel guest accommodation sits under SL_45_10 Living spaces (21 verbatim leaf codes confirmed NBS Source 2026-06-06); restaurants/bars/dining sit under SL_40_20 Dining spaces (8 verbatim codes); food-prep kitchens under SL_35_60 Food management spaces (6 verbatim codes); fitness/pool under SL_42_40 Indoor activity spaces + SL_42_85 Swimming spaces; sanitary under SL_35_80 Sanitary spaces; storage/back-of-house under SL_90_50 Storage spaces + SL_90_20 Common spaces. This multi-subgroup traversal covers: SL_45_10 (guest accommodation — primary representative group, hence _parent_code), SL_40_20 (dining + bars), SL_35_60 (food management / commercial kitchens), SL_35_80 (sanitary), SL_42_40 (fitness), SL_42_85 (pool/spa pools), SL_90_10 (circulation / lobby), SL_90_20 (common / breakout / staff), SL_90_50 (storage / luggage / linen). NBS-sourced canonical_ids use the verbatim NBS leaf under the most representative group. Engineering-consensus entries (hospitality sub-types not individually enumerated in Uniclass v1.35) cite CIBSE TM50:2014 (Lighting in Hospitality Buildings) §3 as primary engineering authority, supplemented by BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.34 hotels and §5.39 restaurants, BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction (2020 edition) adjacency norms, UK Hotel Industry Standards (UKH / BHA), and Approved Document M:2015 §3.22 accessible bedroom provisions. cross_references.cibse_lg + nrm2 stay null per Sprint Z deferral; building_type_codes back-filled at Z.D.1.

entries._inference_note: NBS Source SL_45_10_37 'Hotel rooms' is the verbatim leaf code under SL_45_10 Living spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Sub-type differentiation (standard vs suite vs family vs accessible) follows CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.2 guest-room illuminance categories and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §4.1 room-type matrix.

NBS Source SL_45_10_37 Hotel rooms is the verbatim parent leaf; suite sub-type not individually enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Suite designation follows CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.2 (suite illuminance 150 lux general + 300 lux task) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §4.2 suite-room area minima (42m² junior suite).

NBS Source SL_45_10_37 Hotel rooms is the verbatim parent leaf; family sub-type not individually enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Family-room classification follows CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.2 guest-room illuminance targets and UK Hotel Industry Standards (UKH 2019) §2.3 minimum family-room provisions (separate sleeping zones for adults and children).

NBS Source SL_45_10_37 Hotel rooms is the verbatim parent leaf; accessibility sub-type not individually enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Accessible-room requirements follow Approved Document M:2015 §3.22 Category 3 wheelchair-user bedrooms (minimum 3.6m × 3.9m sleeping zone + 1200mm turning circle) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.2 lighting for mobility-impaired guests.

NBS Source SL_45_10_37 Hotel rooms is the verbatim parent leaf; penthouse/presidential-suite sub-type not enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Classification follows BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §4.3 signature-suite area norms (≥100m² gross internal area) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.2 luxury-tier illuminance layering guidance.

NBS Source SL_35_80_08 'Bathrooms' is the verbatim leaf code under SL_35_80 Sanitary spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel en-suite application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.3 guest bathroom illuminance (200–300 lux at mirror) and BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.34 hotel sanitary-space lighting.

NBS Source SL_35_80_89 'Toilets' is the verbatim leaf code under SL_35_80 Sanitary spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Powder-room sub-type (WC without bath/shower) follows CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.3 sanitary-space illuminance and BS 6465-1:2006+A1:2009 §5 plumbing provisions for single-WC hotel ancillaries.

NBS Source SL_90_10_51 'Lobbies' is the verbatim leaf code under SL_90_10 Circulation spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-lobby application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.1 entrance and circulation illuminance (200 lux general, 300 lux reception desk) and BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.34 hotel entrance lighting.

NBS Source SL_90_20_16 'Customer service counter areas' is the verbatim leaf code under SL_90_20 Common spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-concierge application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.1 (300 lux maintained at check-in counter) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §3.2 front-of-house desk adjacency norms.

NBS Source SL_90_20_08 Breakout spaces is the closest verbatim leaf under SL_90_20; hotel business-centre sub-type not individually enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Classification follows CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.5 (500 lux maintained for business-centre task lighting) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §5.1 business-lounge area norms.

NBS Source SL_45_10_49 'Living rooms' is the verbatim leaf under SL_45_10 Living spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-guest-lounge application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.4 (150 lux general ambient, 300 lux reading task) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §3.3 lounge area norms.

NBS Source SL_40_20_42 'Indoor dining rooms' is the verbatim leaf under SL_40_20 Dining spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Breakfast-room application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (200 lux maintained for buffet breakfast service) and BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.39 restaurant illuminance targets.

NBS Source SL_42_40_30 'Fitness rooms' is the verbatim leaf under SL_42_40 Indoor activity spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-fitness-room application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.8 leisure illuminance (300 lux maintained) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §6.1 minimum fitness-area provisions.

NBS Source SL_35_80_00 Ablutions rooms is the closest verbatim leaf under SL_35_80 Sanitary spaces; spa-treatment sub-type not individually enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Classification follows CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.8 (100–200 lux dimmable for spa ambiance) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §6.2 spa-treatment room area minima (≥12m²).

NBS Source SL_90_50_16 'Cleaners' stores' is the verbatim leaf under SL_90_50 Storage spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Housekeeping-room application per BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §7.1 back-of-house service-corridor norms (one housekeeping bay per 10–15 guest rooms) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 back-of-house illuminance (300 lux).

NBS Source SL_90_50_14 'Clothes stores' is the verbatim leaf under SL_90_50 Storage spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-linen-store application per BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §7.1 (clean linen store ≥2m² per 10 guest rooms) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 back-of-house illuminance (300 lux).

NBS Source SL_90_50_47 'Left luggage stores' is the verbatim leaf under SL_90_50 Storage spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-luggage-store application per BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §3.4 front-of-house luggage adjacency norms and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 back-of-house illuminance (200 lux).

NBS Source SL_90_20_85 'Staff rooms' is the verbatim leaf under SL_90_20 Common spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Staff-canteen application per BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §7.3 staff welfare provisions (minimum 1.1m² per staff member) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 back-of-house illuminance (300 lux).

NBS Source SL_35_60_14 'Commercial kitchens' is the verbatim leaf under SL_35_60 Food management spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Banqueting-kitchen application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.7 (500 lux maintained over preparation surfaces) and BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.36 commercial kitchen illuminance targets.

NBS Source SL_35_60_30 'Food preparation areas' is the verbatim leaf under SL_35_60 Food management spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Dishwash-area application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.7 (300 lux maintained) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §7.2 back-of-house kitchen layout norms (dishwash segregated from clean food prep).

NBS Source SL_40_20_42 'Indoor dining rooms' is the verbatim leaf under SL_40_20 Dining spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Restaurant-dining application per BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.39 (200 lux general + 300 lux table accent) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 restaurant illuminance layering strategy.

NBS Source SL_40_20_42 Indoor dining rooms is the verbatim parent leaf; fine-dining sub-type not individually enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Sub-type follows CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (50–100 lux dimmable ambient + >300 lux focused table accent for fine-dining atmosphere) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §5.3 fine-dining area norms (≥1.8m² per cover).

NBS Source SL_40_20_43 'Informal dining spaces' is the verbatim leaf under SL_40_20 Dining spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Casual-dining application per BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.39 restaurant illuminance targets and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (200 lux general for all-day casual dining).

NBS Source SL_20_50_32 'Food and drink outlets' is the verbatim leaf under SL_20_50 Commercial spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Cafe-seating application per BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.39 (300 lux for cafe counter service) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (200 lux general ambient for cafe seating area).

NBS Source SL_40_20_09 'Beverage stations' is the verbatim leaf under SL_40_20 Dining spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Coffee-bar application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (300 lux task over counter) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §3.5 lobby-coffee-bar adjacency norms.

NBS Source SL_40_20_06 'Bars' is the verbatim leaf under SL_40_20 Dining spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Cocktail-bar application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (50–100 lux dimmable ambient for evening bar atmosphere) and BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.39 drinking establishment illuminance guidance.

NBS Source SL_40_20_06 Bars is the verbatim parent leaf; pub/traditional-bar sub-type not individually enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Sub-type differentiation follows CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (100–200 lux pub general ambient) and BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.39 bar and drinking-establishment illuminance targets.

NBS Source SL_40_20_06 Bars is the verbatim parent leaf; wine-bar sub-type not individually enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Sub-type differentiation follows CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (50–150 lux dimmable for intimate wine-bar atmosphere) and BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.39 bar illuminance guidance.

NBS Source SL_40_20_59 'Outdoor dining areas' is the verbatim leaf under SL_40_20 Dining spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. ifc_space_type=EXTERNAL for open-air terrace per ISO 16739. Application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (50 lux minimum maintained on outdoor hospitality terraces) and BS EN 12464-2:2014 §5.2 outdoor entertainment-area illuminance.

NBS Source SL_40_20_22 'Dining halls' is the verbatim leaf under SL_40_20 Dining spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Banquet-hall application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (300 lux for banqueting service, 100 lux for dining ambiance) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §5.2 banqueting-suite area norms (≥1.5m² per seated cover for formal banquet).

NBS Source SL_40_20_27 'Enclosed dining areas' is the verbatim leaf under SL_40_20 Dining spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Wedding-function-room application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 (100–300 lux dimmable for ceremony and dining) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §5.4 licensed-venue provisions.

NBS Source SL_90_20_08 'Breakout spaces' is the verbatim leaf under SL_90_20 Common spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Conference-room application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.5 (500 lux maintained for conference/meeting use) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §5.1 conference-floor area norms (≥2m² per delegate cabaret style).

NBS Source SL_90_20_08 Breakout spaces is the verbatim parent leaf; hospitality-function breakout sub-type not individually enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Sub-type differentiation follows CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.5 (300–500 lux task for breakout working areas) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §5.1 event-floor breakout-to-conference ratio norms (1 breakout per 50 conference delegates).

NBS Source SL_42_85_41 'Indoor swimming pools' is the verbatim leaf under SL_42_85 Swimming spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-pool application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.8 (300 lux maintained poolside) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §6.1 leisure-pool area norms. Also referenced: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.38 swimming-pool illuminance.

NBS Source SL_42_85_80 'Spa pools' is the verbatim leaf under SL_42_85 Swimming spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Spa-pool application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.8 (100–200 lux dimmable for hydrotherapy/spa pool ambiance) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §6.2 thermal-suite provisions.

NBS Source SL_90_20_13 'Changing rooms' is the verbatim leaf under SL_90_20 Common spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Leisure-changing-room application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.8 (200 lux maintained) and BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §6.1 poolside-changing provision norms.

NBS Source SL_35_80_03 'Accessible toilets' is the verbatim leaf under SL_35_80 Sanitary spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-public-accessible-WC application per Approved Document M:2015 §3.20 accessible WC provision in hotel public areas and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 sanitary illuminance (200 lux).

NBS Source SL_35_80_68 'Public toilets' is the verbatim leaf under SL_35_80 Sanitary spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-public-toilet application per BS 6465-1:2006+A1:2009 §4 (minimum 1 WC per 100 covers for licensed premises) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 (200 lux maintained for public sanitary facilities).

NBS Source SL_90_50_29 'Food stores' is the verbatim leaf under SL_90_50 Storage spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-food-store application per BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §7.2 (minimum 0.04m² per cover for dry-goods storage) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 back-of-house storage illuminance (200 lux).

NBS Source SL_90_50_45 'Keg stores' is the verbatim leaf under SL_90_50 Storage spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Bar-cellar / keg-store application per BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §7.2 cellar provisions and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 (100 lux maintained in storage cellars per Health and Safety at Work Act 1974 minimum).

NBS Source SL_90_50_98 'Wine cellars' is the verbatim leaf under SL_90_50 Storage spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-wine-cellar application per BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §7.2 and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 (100 lux low-UV lamp in wine cellars to prevent label degradation).

NBS Source SL_90_50_36 'Goods stores' is the verbatim leaf under SL_90_50 Storage spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Goods-receiving application per BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §7.1 back-of-house logistics (goods receiving segregated from staff and guest routes) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 (200 lux maintained for safe goods handling).

NBS Source SL_40_20_28 'Food courts' is the verbatim leaf under SL_40_20 Dining spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Food-court application per BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.39 (300 lux general for food-court seating) and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.6 food-court illuminance guidance.

NBS Source SL_35_60_78 'Servery areas' is the verbatim leaf under SL_35_60 Food management spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hotel-servery application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.7 (400 lux maintained at service counters) and BS EN 12464-1:2021 §5.36 commercial kitchen / servery illuminance targets.

NBS Source SL_45_10_24 'Dormitories' is the verbatim leaf under SL_45_10 Living spaces. Confirmed on NBS primary mirror 2026-06-06. Hostel-dormitory application per CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.2 shared-accommodation illuminance and UK Hotel Industry Standards (UKH 2019) §2.6 budget / hostel room provisions.

NBS Source SL_40_20_22 Dining halls is the verbatim parent leaf; staff-dining sub-type not individually enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Sub-type differentiation follows BCO Hotel Industry Guide to Construction 2020 §7.3 staff-welfare area norms and CIBSE TM50:2014 §3.9 (300 lux back-of-house dining). Distinguished from staff_canteen_hotel (SL_90_20_85) which maps to a staff-room context; this entry maps to a dedicated staff dining hall in large hotel properties.
